regulations.title: USECHH 2000
regulations.full_title: Occupational Safety and Health (Use and Standard of Exposure of Chemicals Hazardous to Health) Regulations 2000

questions.question: Do you have a Chemical Register?
questions.detail: A written inventory listing all hazardous chemicals used, stored, or handled at your workplace.
action_steps: Identify all hazardous chemicals in your workplace
Record each chemical's name, quantity, and location
Include supplier details and SDS references
Assign responsibility for keeping the register updated

questions.question: Have you conducted a Chemical Health Risk Assessment (CHRA)?
questions.detail: A written assessment of risks to health from each hazardous chemical used in your workplace.
action_steps: Appoint a registered CHRA assessor (registered with DOSH)
Assess each work process involving hazardous chemicals
Document findings: exposure routes, risk levels, control measures
Review assessment whenever processes change

questions.question: Do you have Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for all chemicals?
questions.detail: Up-to-date SDS from suppliers for every hazardous chemical, accessible to all workers.
action_steps: Request current SDS from suppliers for all chemicals
Organize SDS in a central register (physical or digital)
Ensure SDS are in Bahasa Malaysia and English
Review SDS annually or whenever chemical composition changes

questions.question: Are all chemical containers properly labeled?
questions.detail: Every container must clearly show the chemical name, hazard pictograms, and risk/safety phrases.
action_steps: Label all original and transferred containers immediately
Use standard hazard pictograms and warning text
Include chemical name, supplier info, and date
Replace damaged or faded labels promptly

questions.question: Have you identified the Permissible Exposure Limits (PEL) for each chemical?
questions.detail: Know the ceiling limit and 8-hour time-weighted average for each chemical from Schedule I / Jadual I.
action_steps: Refer to Schedule I / Jadual I for the PEL of each chemical
Document ceiling limits and 8-hour TWA values
Compare against actual workplace exposure levels
Update whenever new chemicals are introduced

questions.question: Do you conduct exposure monitoring at your workplace?
questions.detail: Airborne concentration monitoring to verify exposure levels remain below PEL limits.
action_steps: Identify monitoring points based on CHRA findings
Use validated sampling methods (personal air sampling)
Engage a DOSH-registered monitoring agency if required
Document results and compare against PEL limits

questions.question: Do you provide health surveillance for exposed workers?
questions.detail: Medical surveillance for workers exposed to chemicals listed in Schedule II / Jadual II (e.g. lead, asbestos, cadmium).
action_steps: Identify workers exposed to Schedule II chemicals
Engage a registered Occupational Health Doctor (OHD)
Conduct initial and periodic medical examinations
Maintain confidential health records for each worker

questions.question: Are engineering controls in place to minimize exposure?
questions.detail: Local Exhaust Ventilation (LEV), enclosures, or other mechanical controls to reduce airborne contaminants.
action_steps: Install LEV systems for processes generating dust/fumes/vapors
Ensure LEV design meets recognized standards
Commission and test LEV before use
Schedule regular maintenance and keep inspection records

questions.question: Is appropriate PPE provided and used?
questions.detail: Respirators, gloves, goggles, or protective clothing where engineering controls are insufficient.
action_steps: Select PPE based on CHRA findings and chemical hazards
Provide approved PPE at no cost to workers
Train workers on correct use, fitting, and maintenance
Enforce PPE usage through supervision and disciplinary policy

questions.question: Are warning signs posted in chemical handling areas?
questions.detail: Visible safety signs at entry points and work areas warning of chemical hazards.
action_steps: Identify all chemical storage and handling areas
Install warning signs with hazard symbols in Bahasa Malaysia and English
Include PPE requirements on each sign
Replace faded or damaged signs immediately

questions.question: Do workers receive chemical safety training?
questions.detail: Workers must be trained on chemical hazards, safe handling, emergency procedures, and proper PPE use.
action_steps: Develop a chemical safety training program
Train all workers handling chemicals before they start
Conduct refresher training annually
Document attendance and training content

questions.question: Are all required records maintained and accessible?
questions.detail: CHRA reports, monitoring results, health records, maintenance logs, and training records must be kept.
action_steps: Establish a record-keeping system for all compliance documents
Retain health surveillance records for minimum 30 years
Retain monitoring records for minimum 30 years
Ensure records are available for DOSH inspection upon request
